req.title: Procedure record (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case

"""
Pytest test suite for the requirement:
"A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter."

Covers:
- Happy-path recording
- Permission / role denial
- Missing mandatory elements
- Conflicting state transitions
- Edge cases (duplicate, cancelled encounter, unknown encounter, etc.)
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from uuid import uuid4

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / service layer (thin stubs so the tests are self-contained)
# These would normally be imported from your application package.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PermissionError(Exception):
    pass


class ValidationError(Exception):
    pass


class StateConflictError(Exception):
    pass


class NotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class DuplicateError(Exception):
    pass


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def clinician_user():
    """A user with the 'clinician' role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = str(uuid4())
    user.role = "clinician"
    user.has_permission = MagicMock(return_value=True)
    return user


@pytest.fixture()
def nurse_user():
    """A user with 'nurse' role – can view but NOT record procedures."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = str(uuid4())
    user.role = "nurse"
    user.has_permission = MagicMock(return_value=False)
    return user

    user.has_permission = MagicMock(return_value=False)
    return user

```python
"""
Pytest test suite for the requirement:
"A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter."

Covers:
- Happy-path recording
- Permission / role denial
- Missing mandatory elements
- Conflicting state transitions
- Edge cases (duplicate, cancelled encounter, unknown encounter, etc.)
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from uuid import uuid4

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / service layer (thin stubs so the tests are self-contained)
# These would normally be imported from your application package.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PermissionError(Exception):
    pass


class ValidationError(Exception):
    pass


class StateConflictError(Exception):
    pass


class NotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class DuplicateError(Exception):
    pass


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def clinician_user():
    """A user with the 'clinician' role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = str(uuid4())
    user.role = "clinician"
    user.has_permission = MagicMock(return_value=True)
    return user


@pytest.fixture()
def nurse_user():
    """A user with 'nurse' role – can view but NOT record procedures."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = str(uuid4())
    user.role = "nurse"
    user.has_permission = MagicMock(return_value=False)
    return user


@pytest.fixture()
def admin_user():
    """An administrative user with no clinical write permissions."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = str(uuid4())
    user.role = "admin"
    user.has_permission = MagicMock(return_value=False)
    return user


@pytest.fixture()
def active_encounter():
    """An encounter in 'in-progress' state."""
    encounter = MagicMock()
    encounter.id = str(uuid4())
    encounter.status = "in-progress"
    encounter.patient_id = str(uuid4())
    encounter.start_time = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1)
    encounter.end_time = None
    return encounter


@pytest.fixture()
def finished_encounter():
    """An encounter in 'finished' state."""
    encounter = MagicMock()
    encounter.id = str(uuid4())
    encounter.status = "finished"
    encounter.patient_id = str(uuid4())
    encounter.start_time = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=3)
    encounter.end_time = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1)
    return encounter


@pytest.fixture()
def cancelled_encounter():
    """An encounter that was cancelled."""
    encounter = MagicMock()
    encounter.id = str(uuid4())
    encounter.status = "cancelled"
    encounter.patient_id = str(uuid4())
    return encounter


@pytest.fixture()
def valid_procedure_payload(active_encounter, clinician_user):
    """Minimal valid payload for recording a procedure."""
    return {
        "encounter_id": active_encounter.id,
        "patient_id": active_encounter.patient_id,
        "code": "80146002",          # SNOMED CT – Appendectomy
        "display": "Appendectomy",
        "status": "completed",
        "performed_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        "recorded_by": clinician_user.id,
    }


@pytest.fixture()
def procedure_service(active_encounter, finished_encounter, cancelled_encounter):
    """
    A mock of the ProcedureService that enforces the domain rules we are testing.
    In a real project this would be the actual service under test; here we
    replicate just enough behaviour to make every assertion meaningful.
    """
    service = MagicMock()

    def _record_procedure(payload: dict, requesting_user):
        # 1. Permission check
        if not requesting_user.has_permission("procedure:write"):
            raise PermissionError(
                f"User role '{requesting_user.role}' lacks procedure:write permission."
            )
